About This Report

This is SG Logistics AS's first sustainability report, prepared in accordance with the VSME Basic Module (EFRAG). Reporting period: 1 January – 31 December 2025.

The company is a Norwegian private limited liability company, not currently subject to CSRD, but has chosen voluntary disclosure for greater transparency to customers, suppliers, employees, and other stakeholders.

No external assurance has been applied. As this is the first reporting year, comparative data from prior periods is not available for all disclosures.

SG Logistics AS VSME Basic Sustainability Report 2025 ●
Nils Erik Bjørkedal
Chief Executive Officer · SG Logistics AS
Message from the CEO

Dear Customers,
Partners &
Colleagues

At SG Logistics, relationships are at the center of how we operate. Our role is not only to move goods efficiently, but to be a reliable logistics partner that customers can trust in a changing market environment, with a commitment to contributing to a better environment.

In 2025, we continued to focus on what matters most to our customers: responsiveness, transparency, operational stability, and long-term cooperation. As supply chains become more complex and sustainability expectations increase across Europe, we see responsible business practices as part of delivering quality service — not as a separate initiative.

As a logistics intermediary, our impact is closely connected to the transport solutions we arrange on behalf of our customers. This report is an important step in improving how we measure, understand, and communicate that impact. We strengthened our reporting structure, continued our work on ethical business practices, and maintained a strong internal culture built on trust, accountability, and collaboration.

Looking ahead, we aim to further improve the quality of our sustainability data, strengthen cooperation with transport providers on emission transparency, and develop more efficient logistics solutions that support both operational and environmental performance.

Nils Erik Bjørkedal
CEO, SG Logistics AS

General Information

Basis for Preparation

This is SG Logistics AS's first sustainability report, covering 1 January to 31 December 2025. Prepared in accordance with the VSME Basic Module (EFRAG). The company is a Norwegian private limited liability company, not currently subject to CSRD, but has chosen voluntary disclosure to provide greater transparency to customers, suppliers, employees, and other stakeholders. No external assurance has been applied.

B1 — Company Overview

NACE CodeH – 52.2 Support activities for transportation

As this is the first reporting year under VSME, comparative data from prior periods is not available for all disclosures.

B2 — Sustainability Commitment & Business Model

2025 marks SG Logistics' first VSME Basic sustainability report — an important step toward structured, comparable reporting. Since 2023, the company has provided customers with transport carbon footprint data, free of charge.

Business Model

SG Logistics AS is an independent Norwegian freight forwarder established in Ålesund in 2005. It operates as a non-asset logistics intermediary, coordinating domestic and international transport across road, sea, air, and project logistics. The business model is built on customer proximity, operational flexibility, and long-term relationships.

SG Logistics ensures continuity and operational understanding by allowing the same employee to manage the full customer process, from inquiry and booking to follow-up and invoicing. Customer responsibility is assigned based on transport mode and product area expertise. The company's value chain consists of sourcing transport capacity through external carriers, managing bookings and documentation, and supporting customers throughout the transport process.

Over 99% of total GHG emissions arise indirectly from subcontracted freight transport — particularly international air freight arranged on behalf of customers.

SG Logistics AS VSME Basic Sustainability Report 2025 ●
General Information B2 · Value Chain
SG Logistics AS

Our Value Chain

As a non-asset logistics broker, SG Logistics sits between customers and carriers. The company does not own vehicles or transport infrastructure — its value is in coordination, expertise, and relationships. This structure means over 99% of GHG emissions arise in subcontracted carrier operations (Scope 3 Category 4), making carrier engagement the primary lever for reducing the company's environmental footprint.

Environmental B3 · Energy & GHG Emissions
SG Logistics AS

GHG Emissions 2025

Scope 1 — Direct
0.00tCO₂e

No owned vehicles, no fossil fuel combustion on site.

Scope 2 — Electricity
0.00tCO₂e

Operational control approach — electricity contracted by building owner.

Scope 3 — Value Chain
4,892tCO₂e

99.67% from subcontracted carrier operations (Cat 4).

Scope 3 Breakdown

Scope 3 Category Source Basis tCO₂e
Cat 3 — Fuel & energy-relatedEV upstream electricity (WTT)–0.12
Cat 4 — Upstream transportFreight for clients (EcoTransit WTW)–4,876.28
Cat 6 — Business travelAviation, 3 routes, 25 passengers, incl. RFIDEFRA 202414.57
Cat 8 — Upstream leased assetsBuilding electricity 110,615 kWhNVE 20241.32
Total — All Scopes4,892.29
Methodology: GHG Protocol Corporate Standard. GWP from IPCC AR6 (100-year). Aviation includes RFI (DEFRA 2024: 0.255 kg CO₂e/pkm). Scope 2 zero under operational control — electricity contracted by building owner.

Why Cat 4 Dominates

Category 4 accounts for 99.67% of total emissions. SG Logistics arranges transport on behalf of clients but does not own or operate vehicles. The emissions arise in subcontracted carriers' operations — structurally inherent to non-asset freight forwarding.

Reduction efforts focus on carrier selection, modal shift, and route optimisation — the primary levers available to a non-asset broker.

12,769
Shipments arranged in 2025
Covering 51.8 million km
Known limitation: Employee commuting (Cat 7) not calculated for 2025 — no data collection in place. A staff travel survey is planned for 2026.
SG Logistics AS VSME Basic Sustainability Report 2025 ●
Environmental B4 · B5 · B6 · B7
SG Logistics AS

Environmental Disclosures

B4 — Pollution of Air, Water and Soil

Not applicable. SG Logistics operates from a single leased office with no production facilities, industrial installations, fuel storage, or infrastructure generating regulated pollutant emissions. Pollution from subcontracted carriers falls within their own operational boundaries. This will be reviewed if the operational footprint changes.

B6 — Water

Not applicable. Operations are office-based. Water use is confined to standard sanitary consumption within leased premises, managed by the building owner. No water-intensive processes exist.

B5 — Biodiversity

Not applicable. The office at Breivika Industriveg 63, Ålesund is not located in or adjacent to biodiversity-sensitive areas, Natura 2000 sites, or areas of recognised biodiversity value. Reviewed annually.

B7 — Resource Use, Circular Economy and Waste

Not applicable for the current period. No manufacturing, construction, or packaging activities. Office waste (paper, consumables) is negligible. No hazardous waste. Consistent with Scope 3 Category 5 assessed as not material. Will be included in full if the company expands into warehousing or asset-based operations.
All four disclosures (B4–B7) will be reviewed annually under the operational control approach applied consistently across this report.
SG Logistics AS VSME Basic Sustainability Report 2025 ●
Social B8 · B9 · B10
SG Logistics AS

Workforce Disclosures

B8 — General Workforce Characteristics

At 31 December 2025, SG Logistics employed 15 people (15 FTE), all on permanent full-time contracts in Norway. No temporary contracts, agency workers, or self-employed workers were engaged during the reporting period.

Turnover rate and gender pay gap are below mandatory VSME Basic disclosure thresholds (50 and 150 employees respectively) and are not required.

B9 — Health & Safety

SG Logistics operates a statutory HMS management system under the Internkontrollforskriften, covering 100% of the workforce.

Recordable Accidents
0
Fatalities
0

B10 — Remuneration, Bargaining & Training

All employees are paid at or above Norwegian statutory minimum wage. The company has a tariff agreement with NHO/Negotia (Funksjonæravtalen), entered September 2024. Three of fifteen employees (20%) are union members.

Tariff Agreement

NHO/Negotia · Funksjonæravtalen · Since September 2024

Average annual training hours were not collected in 2025.
SG Logistics AS VSME Basic Sustainability Report 2025 ●
Social S2 · Workers in the Value Chain (Voluntary)
SG Logistics AS

Value Chain Workers

As a non-asset freight forwarder, over 99% of GHG emissions arise from carrier operations. The working conditions and sustainability practices of transport providers are therefore directly material to SG Logistics' sustainability performance. This voluntary S2 disclosure reflects that materiality.

Policies

A supplier code of conduct, adapted for the transport sector, has been signed by two suppliers representing approximately 40% of transport spend. The company's code of conduct states it only engages with parties whose ethical standards are equivalent to its own.

No formal supplier audit or due diligence process was in place during 2025. This is an identified area for development.

Supplier Survey 2025

First structured supplier survey conducted in 2025 (Norwegian and English). No grievances reported.

Commercial Relationship (A)
4.51/5
Communication (B)
4.52/5
Ethical Conduct (C)
4.63/5
Overall Average
4.55/5

Supplier Sustainability Capabilities

Of the responding suppliers:

78%
Have a written code of conduct and HMS system
44%
Hold ISO 14001 environmental certification
44%
Already report GHG emissions across some scopes
44%
Can provide per-shipment emission data on request
78%
Subcontract transport; 6 of 7 require their subcontractors to meet own labour and safety standards

All respondents confirmed SG Logistics behaves ethically, that they were not pressured to act unethically, and that they would feel safe raising concerns. Zero ethical concerns in open-text responses.

Targets: No formal targets set for 2025. Target-setting planned for the 2026 cycle.
SG Logistics AS VSME Basic Sustainability Report 2025 ●
Governance B11 · Corruption & Bribery
SG Logistics AS

Governance Disclosures

B11 — Convictions and Fines for Corruption and Bribery

SG Logistics has not received any fines or convictions for violations of anti-corruption or anti-bribery legislation during the reporting period.

0
Corruption Convictions
0
Bribery Fines
0
Whistleblowing Reports

Our Ethical Commitment

SG Logistics' code of conduct includes zero tolerance for corruption, fraud, and any form of unethical conduct. The code applies to all employees, board members, and parties acting on behalf of the company.

Employees may report concerns through the company's whistleblowing procedure (varsling) under Arbeidsmiljøloven §2A. Anonymous reporting is accepted, and reporters are protected against retaliation. The channel is also accessible to external parties including suppliers and customers.

The supplier code of conduct explicitly requires equivalent ethical standards from carriers. During 2025, zero supplier grievances or reports were received through any channel.
